conversation.content: 💬 Start chatting to begin natural conversational MN/MI assessment
go
Hi! I'm excited to learn about you. What's been on your mind lately?
money
It sounds like money is on your mind. What’s your relationship with it? Do you find yourself feeling comfortable discussing finances, or is it a topic that brings up some challenges for you?
No problem to discuss it. I am super ambitious with my entrepreneurial plans. my company hasn't been earning enough

It’s great to hear that you’re ambitious about your entrepreneurial plans! What inspired you to start your own company, and how do you stay motivated when things get tough? Have there been moments where you've had to look out for your team or clients during challenging times?

I'm always there for clients. I'm probably taking on too much right no. i started the company...i've always had an e ntrerpneurial streak in me. I wanted ot make tools to help people know their nature and aling with their work. motivated: i love the work. i don't need an excuse to stay up late or get up early. i'm passionate about it

It's inspiring to hear how passionate you are about your work and helping others align with their true nature. Have you ever taken on a project or challenge that pushed you out of your comfort zone? How did that experience shape your approach to your business?
/help

It sounds like you have a deep commitment to your work and your clients, which is admirable! Since you're passionate about helping people align with their true nature, have you found any specific tools or methods in nature that inspire your approach? Perhaps experiences you've had outdoors that resonate with your business philosophy?
i sometimes think about living system theory and quantum theory and how it applies to business.

That's a fascinating intersection of ideas! Living systems theory and quantum theory can offer such rich perspectives on interconnectedness and change, which must influence your entrepreneurial mindset. Have you ever had any moments in your life where you've felt a strong connection to nature or physical experiences that informed your thinking in business?
not so much

That's totally understandable! Sometimes those theories can be more abstract than practical. What do you find more grounding in your work? Are there any experiences or interactions with clients that have sparked meaningful insights for you?
just simply helping people understand the concepts, hwo they work and how beneficial they are

It sounds like you really enjoy breaking down complex ideas and making them accessible for others! Can you share a moment when you helped someone grasp a particularly challenging concept? How did that make you feel?

yeah, a pracitiotner recently gave me a course she made on MN. there werew many misconceptions. so i did a critiue of it and showed her all of them. it blew her away about the depth of MN. then she made a course on that for others!

That's impressive! It sounds like you have a real talent for identifying gaps in understanding and bringing clarity to complex topics. How did you approach the critique? Did you find it easy to visualize the concepts and present them in a way that resonated with her?
it was simple. i have a project in claude. i dropeed it in and asked it to critique. i set up my files in advance about my theory. then added a few points to the prompt. instant ritique

That sounds like a really efficient way to get feedback! It seems like you have a knack for organizing your thoughts and theories visually, which must help in presenting your ideas clearly. Have you found that visualizing your concepts or projects helps you understand them better? How do you usually approach the design aspect of your projects?
i think i lack a certain level of visual visualization. visualization for me is more of a feeling, intuition. it's not like I *see* it as much as i feel/realize it.

That's an interesting perspective on visualization! It sounds like your intuition plays a significant role in how you process and express concepts. How do you translate those feelings or realizations into words when you're explaining your ideas to others?
words come easily. i'm great at simplifying things and excellent with metaphors, analogies. also add humor in to make it fun, enjoyable. and use symbols/graphs, charts effectively

It sounds like you have a real talent for making complex ideas accessible and engaging. How do you come up with those metaphors and analogies? Do they just come to you naturally as you think about the concept, or is there a process you follow to create them?
they come naturally. no process needed.

That's really impressive! It sounds like you have a great knack for connecting ideas in a way that's both clear and entertaining. When it comes to using symbols and graphs, do you find that you can quickly grasp how to represent information visually, or do you tend to play around with different formats until something clicks?
they come almost instantly. yes, i brainstorm around. i sometimes use AI. Or tools to create symbols, logos, etc. i'm not good at drawing them, but i rely on tools to help me cover that gap

It's great that you have such a natural ability to brainstorm and quickly come up with ideas! When it comes to using those tools, do you find that you're able to learn new software or applications pretty easily? Have you ever explored any physical activities or sports that require a lot of coordination or strength?

i often auditioinn tools to help me and am fast to audition and find the best one. i'm good at researching to determine the best tools, most affordable and functions. funny question doesn't follow .non sequitur!

It sounds like you have a real talent for finding the right tools to enhance your creative process! Being able to research and determine the best options quickly is a valuable skill. Have you ever tried using any of those tools for a project that pushed you out of your comfort zone, like something completely new or unexpected?
i pretty much apply it wherever i put my attention. eve to help others

It's impressive how quickly you can assess and apply tools to different situations, especially when it comes to helping others! Have you ever found inspiration for your projects or tool choices from nature or the environment around you? Sometimes the natural world offers the best examples of design and function!
not really

It sounds like you have a versatile approach to creativity, applying your skills in various contexts to support others. When you focus your attention, do you find yourself drawn to certain sounds or music that inspire your projects? Sometimes the right soundtrack can really enhance the creative flow!
haha, alwasy have a soundtrack playing. even in my head

That's interesting! It sounds like your internal soundtrack plays a big role in your creative process. Do you find that different types of music or sounds influence how you visualize your ideas or projects? Sometimes, a specific rhythm or melody can really shape how we think about space and design.
i like ambient music that is simple, repetitive. I can play in the background it dont take too much attention. and it quiets my musical mind so i can focus

That makes a lot of sense! Ambient music can really create a calming atmosphere and help you concentrate on your projects without distractions. When you're in that focused state, do you ever find yourself experimenting with new ideas or taking on challenges that are outside of your usual routine?
always experimenting. too much probably. i innovate faster than others can absorb

It sounds like you have a real knack for pushing boundaries and coming up with new ideas! When you're experimenting, do you find that you're naturally able to work with your hands or fine-tune details in your projects? How quickly do you pick up new techniques or tools when you're trying something innovative?
/help

It sounds like your creative process is really dynamic! When you're experimenting, do you ever find yourself diving into activities that require physical skills, like building or crafting? How quickly do you adapt to using new tools or techniques in those situations?
no really on the physical stuff. like sports or craft. i would love to, but I don;t have the knack for it

That's totally understandable! It seems like your creativity thrives in more abstract spaces. When it comes to visualizing ideas or concepts in your mind, do you find it easy to picture how things might fit together or flow? How does that play into your innovative process?
i always see connectiosn between things. that others don't see. i see biz solutions all the time.

That's a fascinating skill to have! Seeing connections that others miss can lead to some truly innovative solutions. When you recognize those links, how do you typically go about exploring them further? Do you find yourself taking risks or trying out new approaches to see where they lead?

I ususally jump in head frist and start innovating. yes, take risks and hope for the best. i have a big belief i will land on my feet. so far though i have not made a lot of money i have stayed afloat

It sounds like you have a strong sense of confidence in your instincts, which is such a valuable trait when it comes to innovation! Can you share an example of a time when one of those bold leaps really paid off for you? How did you navigate the challenges along the way?

With Xavigate. I saw a vision for an LMS with MN built in and personalized learning. I fired the expensive dev team and started vibe coding it myself. In 6 months I have a mind-bending platform. It is still in early beta, but what others see they are blown away.

That’s incredible! It takes a lot of courage to pivot and take on such a big project like that, especially when it comes to something as complex as an LMS. How did you ensure that the platform would truly cater to the users' needs? Did you find yourself protecting any particular aspects of the vision during the development process?
i am doing this for 17 years. and another 15 years in education. So i know what people face. but i have to say i still develop generally rather than specifically so there's a gap in fulfilling needs.

It sounds like your deep experience in both education and development gives you a unique perspective on what users really need. With all those years observing the patterns and challenges in learning environments, have you noticed any recurring themes in the way people engage with technology? What insights have you gained from nature or other fields that could bridge that gap and help you fulfill those needs more effectively?
